mechanism_narrative: A 5-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 5-day moving average in periods of rising industrial production (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 20-month decline in housing starts signals a supply-side shock that compresses the S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day moving average in periods of declining housing starts (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 60-day moving average in periods of rising federal funds rates above 4.5% (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 7% signals a weakening labor market that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day moving average in periods of rising unemployment rates above 7% (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in real GDP above 11700 signals a strengthening demand environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 5-day moving average in periods of rising real GDP above 11700 (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 20-month decline in industrial production below 100 signals a supply-side shock that compresses the S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day moving average in periods of declining industrial production below 100 (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% and a 1-month increase in the unemployment rate above 7% signals a tightening monetary cycle and a weakening labor market that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 60-day moving average in periods of rising federal funds rates above 4.5% and unemployment rates above 7% (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in real GDP above 11700 and a 1-month increase in industrial production above 105 signals a strengthening demand environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 5-day moving average in periods of rising real GDP above 11700 and industrial production above 105 (t-test p < 0.05).
